01_initial_request.user_intent: Develop a better and easy-to-use sample preparation method for low-input proteomics to bridge the gap between standard proteomics and single-cell proteomics for in-depth analysis of rare cells
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Identify on average 2,998±106 protein groups from 100 cells and 3,934±85 protein groups from 500 cells

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Rare cell proteomics requires pure cell populations to avoid contamination. [Gap] Standard sorting tubes may cause sample loss. [Decision] To achieve high purity and minimize loss, chose to use BD FACSAria SORP flow cytometer to perform FACS sorting on 293T cells in protein-LoBind tubes with pure PBS sheath liquid, expecting to obtain viable sorted cells.

parameters.collection_tube: Protein-LoBind tubes (0.5 mL, truncated to 0.2 mL)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Desired numbers of 293T cells (10, 100, 500) were sorted and counted with high purity.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sorted cells need to be lysed to release proteins. [Gap] Conventional lysis may lose sample. [Decision] To achieve efficient lysis with minimal loss, chose to use Ice-water ultrasonic bath to perform non-contact intermittent sonication on cells in lysis buffer, expecting to obtain cell lysate.

parameters.lysis_buffer: 20 mM HEPES (pH 7.4), 150 mM NaCl, 600 mM guanidine HCl, 1% DDM, protease inhibitor mixture

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cell lysate was obtained and protein concentration determined by Micro BCA Kit.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] A specialized reactor is needed for low-input sample preparation. [Gap] Existing RCPR used SCX monolith which is impractical for mixed-mode. [Decision] To achieve integrated sample prep, chose to use Customized soldering iron to perform in situ frit formation and packing of C18/SCX/SAX beads in capillary column, expecting to obtain 3-frit mixed-mode RCPR device.

parameters.beads: 3 μm C18, 20 μm POROS SCX and SAX (1:1)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 3-frit mixed-mode RCPR was fabricated with designed lengths of beads (1-5 mm depending on cell count).

references.citation: Yang et al., previously reported RCPR (Ref 18)
references.relevance: Basis for improving the RCPR design by replacing SCX monolith with mixed-mode beads.
Inspiration for mixed-mode mechanism and integrated workflow.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Device design needs optimization for repeatability. [Gap] 2-frit design may allow bead mixing or protein loss. [Decision] To evaluate design, chose to use 2-frit mixed-mode RCPR to perform sample processing on 10 ng 293T lysate, expecting to assess variation and protein identification.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified over 2400 protein groups but variation was large (CV 40-55%).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] 2-frit design showed high variation. [Gap] Need to prevent undigested proteins interacting with C18 resin. [Decision] To improve repeatability, chose to use 3-frit mixed-mode RCPR to perform sample processing on 10 ng 293T lysate, expecting to reduce variation.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified 2421 protein groups with CV reduced to 12% (proteins) and 15% (peptides).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Validated 3-frit RCPR needs to be tested on actual cell counts. [Gap] Performance on 10-500 cells unknown. [Decision] To evaluate performance, chose to use 3-frit mixed-mode RCPR to perform integrated proteomics sample preparation (loading, reduction, digestion, desalting) on FACS-sorted 293T cells, expecting to obtain processed peptides.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Peptides were prepared and transferred to C18 beads within the device.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Prepared peptides need to be analyzed. [Gap] Need to quantify protein identification capability. [Decision] To analyze samples, chose to use Q Exactive HF-X to perform nanoLC-MS analysis in DDA mode, expecting to obtain MS spectra.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: MS raw data generated for 10, 100, and 500 cell samples.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw MS data needs processing for identification. [Gap] Need to convert spectra to protein IDs. [Decision] To process data, chose to use Proteome Discoverer 2.4 to perform database search with Sequest HT, expecting to obtain protein group counts.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified 946, 2998, and 3934 protein groups from 10, 100, and 500 cells respectively.

references.relevance: Previously reported processing method used as baseline.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sensitivity can be improved with newer MS. [Gap] Default parameters may not be optimal for low-input. [Decision] To test baseline, chose to use timsTOF Pro to perform analysis with default DDA parameters on HeLa digest, expecting to assess identification numbers.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified fewer protein groups compared to optimized settings.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Default parameters yielded suboptimal results. [Gap] Need to accumulate more precursors for low-input. [Decision] To improve sensitivity, chose to use timsTOF Pro to perform analysis with optimized DDA parameters (lower intensity threshold, narrowed mass range), expecting to increase protein IDs.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified 3963±44 (10 ng) and 5514±42 (50 ng) protein groups, improved by 39% and 31%.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] timsTOF data requires specific processing. [Gap] Need LFQ for comparison. [Decision] To analyze timsTOF data, chose to use MSFragger with FragPipe to perform database search with match between runs, expecting to obtain quantified protein groups.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Label-free quantified protein groups obtained for hair cell comparison.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Method needs biological application validation. [Gap] Hair cells are rare and hard to profile. [Decision] To demonstrate utility, chose to use BD FACSAria SORP flow cytometer to perform FACS sorting of cochlear hair cells from juvenile and adult mice, expecting to obtain pure hair cells.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 500 FACS-sorted hair cells collected per replicate.

references.relevance: Transgenic mouse model description for hair cell labeling.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hair cell samples require low-input prep. [Gap] Standard methods lose too much sample. [Decision] To prepare hair cell samples, chose to use 3-frit mixed-mode RCPR to perform integrated sample preparation, expecting to obtain digested peptides.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Peptides prepared from juvenile and adult hair cells.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hair cell peptides need high sensitivity analysis. [Gap] Need to compare juvenile vs adult proteomes. [Decision] To analyze hair cells, chose to use timsTOF Pro to perform nanoLC-MS analysis with optimized DDA parameters, expecting to obtain quantitative proteome data.

parameters.samples: Juvenile and Adult mouse hair cells

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MS data acquired with dynamic ranges over 6 orders of magnitude.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteome data needs bioinformatic interpretation. [Gap] Need to identify differentially expressed proteins. [Decision] To interpret data, chose to use MSFragger with FragPipe to perform LFQ and statistical analysis, expecting to obtain DEP lists.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 241 DEPs up-regulated in juvenile, 59 in adult. Calbindin and Myosin 6 quantified.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Data-dependent acquisition (DDA) mode on Q Exactive HF-X and timsTOF Pro

protein_groups_100_cells.interpretation: Matches the quantifiable goal of 2,998±106 protein groups from 100 cells.

protein_groups_500_cells.interpretation: Matches the quantifiable goal of 3,934±85 protein groups from 500 cells.

hair_cells_juvenile.interpretation: Demonstrates applicability to rare primary cells (mouse cochlear hair cells).

hair_cells_adult.interpretation: Demonstrates ability to detect biological differences in low-input samples.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The developed 3-frit mixed-mode RCPR successfully identified the target number of protein groups from 100 and 500 mammalian cells, bridging the gap between standard and single-cell proteomics.
